quote:
When a banquet echoes with thunder, the humblest observer still recognizes that peace must rise from silence.  

Even the mightiest mountain does not choose its stones; the wind decides the path.  

The most powerful hands wield iron only so long as they still carry a gentle heart within their grip.  

Freedom, like a bird freed from a cage, remains only as long as the wind feels unbound.  

When the earth mourns a slow creature, it reminds us that kindness grows in the stillness of stone.  

The map of a battlefield made of ink is far more fragile than one carved in the heart of stone.  

A leader who lifts the fallen does not build towers of stone; he builds bridges of light.
quote_preview: When a banquet echoes with thunder, the humblest observer still recognizes that peace must rise from silence.
